title: Transmission Electron Microscopy of Native Nanodiscs
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8n21u","text":"This is a protocol for conducting transmission electron microscopy of native nanodiscs to determine population size distribution and morphology.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Dilute nanodisc samples into single molecule range, typically around between 1:10 and 1:100 is the samples have been through size exclusion chromatography using dilution buffer ( 50 millimolar (mM), Tris HCl pH 7.4) .
Glow-discharge carbon-coated copper grids (200 mesh) for 00:00:30 seconds.
Apply 5 µL, sample to the grid and after 00:01:00 minute blot off with Whiteman ashless filter paper.
Wash grid once with 5 µL, uranyl formate for 00:00:05 seconds.
Stain grid with 5 µL, uranyl formate for 00:01:00 minute and blot dry with Whiteman ashless filter paper.
Allow to dry for 00:30:00 minutes to Overnight
Take micrographs using a JEOL JEM 1400PLUS electron microscope at an operating voltage of 80 kV.
creator: Caroline Brown
